A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent abandoned the required join with NPM package releases and instead queried only project_info for MIT projects with "fork" in the text (call_oBfQdzwsWQmuQJfCmacGssDL) and parsed forks (call_Nrm25Y5vzl9W987iyr2uu4Vp), leading to results like rails/rails that ignore the NPM and IsRelease constraints.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent used the correct overall approach but joined to project_packageversion without restricting the relationship type (call_hOu0eUEw9QMltxlq4dHpjw1h), producing incorrect package→project mappings (e.g., @docly/web 0.1.371 mapped to mui-org/material-ui in call_RvxZemrM7Zy6YgWKjluTo5QN), which led to an incorrect top-5 list (missing semantic-org/semantic-ui and including tailwindcss/tailwindcss) and wrong versions.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: yes
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent arbitrarily truncated the data with LIMIT 20000 in both the package and project queries (e.g., SELECT ... FROM packageinfo ... LIMIT 20000 and SELECT ... FROM project_info ... LIMIT 20000) and computed the top 5 forks within this incomplete subset, causing it to miss the true highest-fork MIT projects and return incorrect results.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent filtered by MIT in packageinfo (query_db on package_database using Licenses LIKE '%MIT%') and never applied the required 'project license = MIT' filter from project_info in execute_python, so it ranked forks across mismatched projects and returned a top-5 that omitted moment and semantic-ui and included tailwindcss and mono.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@
